name: baoyu-format-markdown
description: Formats plain text or markdown files with frontmatter, titles, summaries, headings, bold, lists, and code blocks. Use when user asks to "format markdown", "beautify article", "add formatting", or improve article layout. Outputs to {filename}-formatted.md.
capabilities: Add or optimize YAML frontmatter with title, slug, summary, and cover image fields
Structure plain text into markdown with headings, bold text, lists, and code blocks
Fix CJK (Chinese/Japanese/Korean) emphasis and spacing issues
Replace ASCII quotes with fullwidth quotes for typography
Format markdown frontmatter YAML for consistency
author: jimliu
securitySummary: Security evaluation confirms this is a legitimate markdown formatting skill. Static findings flagged external command execution (autocorrect-node), filesystem operations, and npm package URLs. All are false positives: execSync runs a known typography tool with CLI-provided paths, file I/O is scoped to user-specified files, and network access is limited to standard npm package downloads.
valueStatement: Transforms plain text and markdown files into well-structured documents with proper frontmatter, typography, and formatting. Outputs clean, publication-ready markdown with CJK language support.
limitations: Does not modify content - only adds formatting
Requires user to specify the source file path
Autocorrect feature requires npx to be available

useCases.title: Blog Post Formatting
useCases.description: Format draft blog posts from plain text or improve existing markdown articles with proper frontmatter, headings, and typography.

useCases.title: Documentation Cleanup
useCases.description: Apply consistent formatting and structure to technical documentation or readme files.

useCases.title: CJK Document Preparation
useCases.description: Fix spacing and emphasis issues in Chinese, Japanese, or Korean mixed-language markdown documents.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Markdown Formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Format the file /path/to/document.md using baoyu-format-markdown skill.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to add markdown formatting to an existing file

promptTemplates.title: Plain Text Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert the plain text file /path/to/draft.txt to well-structured markdown with frontmatter and proper headings.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has plain text they want converted to markdown

promptTemplates.title: Typography Fix Only
promptTemplates.prompt: Run typography fixes only on /path/to/article.md without changing the existing markdown structure.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to fix CJK spacing while keeping original formatting

promptTemplates.title: Full Formatting with Quotes
promptTemplates.prompt: Format /path/to/post.md with all features enabled: frontmatter optimization, typography fixes, and fullwidth quote replacement.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants complete formatting with quote conversion

bestPractices: Always backup original files before formatting as the skill creates new output files
Use the --quotes flag only when fullwidth quote typography is specifically needed
For CJK documents, keep spacing enabled to maintain proper mixed-language formatting
antiPatterns: Do not use on files that already have complex markdown - use 'Keep original formatting' option
Avoid running on very large files as the formatting process processes the entire document
Do not expect content generation - this skill only formats existing content

faq.question: What happens to my original file?
faq.answer: The original file remains unchanged. The formatted output is saved as {filename}-formatted.md.

faq.question: Can I customize the frontmatter fields?
faq.answer: The skill auto-generates title, slug, and summary. You can edit these after formatting or use EXTEND.md for preferences.

faq.question: Does this work with Chinese, Japanese, or Korean text?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes CJK-friendly formatting and can fix emphasis and spacing issues in mixed-language documents.

faq.question: What is the difference between Optimize and Keep Original?
faq.answer: Optimize adds or improves markdown structure. Keep Original preserves your existing formatting and only applies typography fixes.

faq.question: How do I enable fullwidth quote replacement?
faq.answer: Use the --quotes or -q flag when running the script, or select option 1 in the workflow when prompted.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code as supported tools.
